person.title: Ms
person.address-text: Flat 6 Cityscape Apartment, 43 Heneage St, United Kingdom

bng-condition-exemption-reasons.reason: Development subject to the de minimis exemption (development below the threshold)
bng-condition-exemption-reasons.details: The applicant states the works are internal-only development to an existing building, with no demolition, new build, excavation, landscaping, external development, land take or habitat loss. The property retains C3 dwellinghouse use and the proposal includes listed building consent.

checklist.note: No checklist section was visible in the PDF. Values were inferred from the visible supporting document list.

community-consultation.note: No community consultation section was visible in the supplied Planning Portal PDF text or rendered pages.

existing-use.existing-use-details: The site has been vacant since our purchase in March 2025.
existing-use.last-use-details: The building was used as a hostel by the previous owner Shian Housing Association.
existing-use.note: The application description says the use class remained C3 dwellinghouse, while the last use is described as a hostel by the previous owner.

foul-sewage.note: The form references AE101-AE105, AE201, AE202 and A801. Not all of those exact references were visible in the supplied document-list.

hrs-operation.note: The form says hours of opening are not relevant to this proposal.

lb-alter.note: Document references preserve the applicant's references from the form: AE101-AE105 existing plans; AD101-AD105 removal plans; A101-A105 proposed plans; AE201-AE202 existing elevations; A201-A202 proposed elevations; AE301-AE302 existing sections; A301-A302 proposed sections; A901-A903 doors and windows schedule; A904-A905 references. The document-list uses different or abbreviated names for some files, so alignment to portal document rows needs review.

lb-grade.note: The form records Grade II and not ecclesiastical. The listed-building reference itself was not visible on the form.

building-elements.proposed-materials: Allow to install a small metal exhaust, 20cm diameter, finished in matte black, at the rear elevation. See drawing A-202.

building-elements.existing-materials: Timber sash window with single glazed units, frame painted white
building-elements.proposed-materials: Rotten timber areas to be removed, new timber elements grafted in place; all timber elements to be primed and finished with white paint finish; glass to be replaced with slimline double glazed units.

Front door at upper ground floor: solid timber door painted black; back door at upper ground floor: solid timber door with glass, painted black; back door at lower ground floor: solid timber door with glass, painted black. Please refer to drawing A-901 for complete door schedule.

Original timber floorboards to be replaced on a like-for-like basis where missing or damaged. New engineered wood floor coverings to be installed following the removal of modern carpet and vinyl floor coverings, tile floors to be introduced in bathrooms.
Bedroom walls: timber stud and plasterboard painted white. Bathroom walls: finished with large format ceramic tiles.
Various proposed materials. Please refer to A-901 for complete door schedule.

non-res-floorspace.note: The form includes an Existing and Proposed Uses table for C3 dwellinghouses: 241 square metres existing, 0 lost and 0 gained. This has been treated as residential rather than non-residential floorspace.

ownership-certs.note: Two redacted owner/agricultural tenant entries are visible with the same address and notice date.

proposal-details.description: 77 Stoke Newington Church St was used as a social housing/bedsit, while the use class remained C3(dwellinghouse). This application seeks planning permission and listed building consent for internal alterations to sensitively restore the property's historic character while restoring it for sustainable, modern single family use. The works focus on reinstating the original plan form by removing later partitions where sensible, restoring historic features such as fireplaces, cornicing, shutter boxes, and floorboards, and introducing traditionally designed joinery and fittings throughout. Non-original elements, including built-in cupboards and outdated kitchens and bathrooms, will be removed or reconfigured. New kitchens, bathrooms, and storage will be introduced in discreet locations using existing service routes where possible, minimising disruption to the building fabric. A new ensuite and utility area will be incorporated on the upper floors to support contemporary living needs. All existing windows, non-historic and dating from mid-to-late 20th century, will be refurbished or replaced with timber slimline double-glazed units, improving energy performance while respecting the building's appearance. Historic staircases and flooring will be repaired and restored, with sympathetic new finishes applied throughout.

related-applications.note: The combined full planning permission plus listed building consent application includes a Superseded consents question, answered No. No related applications section was visible in the supplied PDF.

res-units.note: The form says no self-contained residential units will be lost or added. Totals are inferred as one existing and one proposed dwellinghouse from the C3 single family use description.

site-area.note: The form gives 169.10 square metres, converted to hectares.

site-locations.address-text: 77 Stoke Newington Church Street, Hackney, Hackney, London

vehicle-parking.note: The form says the site does not have existing vehicle/cycle parking spaces and the proposal will not add or remove any parking spaces.

waste-storage-collection.waste-storage-area-details: Every unit in this proposal has dedicated internal and external storage space for dry recycling, food waste and residual waste.
waste-storage-collection.separate-recycling-arrangements-details: Every unit in this proposal has dedicated internal and external storage space for dry recycling, food waste and residual waste.

additional-properties.notes: Draft extraction from a redacted Planning Portal PDF and a document-list. Direct document download URLs and base64 file content were not available, so document file URLs are blank.
